blockers: Runtime UAT owed for US-024: launch the app, attach >=1 database, select SQL in the federated editor, press Ask AI, confirm the modal says "Refine selected SQL with AI", the returned SQL replaces ONLY the selection, undo (Cmd-Z) restores the pre-refine text in one step, and a non-SELECT result still raises the genWarning banner.

US-024 sends a live prompt to the configured provider; no agent-side live call was made. The refine block wording is copied verbatim from the shipped QueryEditor path, so provider behaviour is inherited, not new — but unobserved on the federated prompt.
summary: US-024 IMPLEMENTED. Lane normal, scope narrowed by the user against the external-provider hard gate. Proof bar GREEN (agent-measured): typecheck exit 0, vitest 146/146 across 14 files (+4 federated refine tests), build exit 0. FederatedQueryTab gained the selection-refine mode QueryEditor already had. Shipped: buildFederatedUserMessageParts/buildFederatedUserMessage take an optional baseSql and emit the same "Existing query to modify (return the FULL updated query, not a fragment)" block + "Change requested:" label as buildUserMessageParts; AiGenerateFederatedSqlPayload gains optional baseSql; the AI_GENERATE_FEDERATED_SQL handler threads it through; FederatedQueryTab grew aiBaseSql state + aiSelRef + openAiModal, dispatching the result into the selected range through the live EditorView so undo history survives, falling back to setSql when there is no selection. NO new IPC channel — one channel carries both modes, exactly as AI_GENERATE_SQL does. Key invariant, pinned by test rather than asserted: when baseSql is absent/empty/whitespace the federated prompt is byte-for-byte what it was, so the shipped generate path cannot regress; and the base block lives ONLY in the request tail, never in schemaContext, because schemaContext is the cached prefix (client.ts). The base-SQL preview uses the theme-neutral rgba(127,127,127,0.12) of TroubleshootPanel rather than antd token lookups, since FederatedQueryTab has no useToken() and ADR 0011 discourages fresh inline token styling. PREVIOUS FEATURE PARKED: troubleshoot-sql-error (US-020) is still execution-complete-pending-uat with its three blockers preserved under parked_features.
next_action: Runtime UAT of US-024 by the user (see blockers). Then clear the parked troubleshoot-sql-error feature: its own UAT, docs/decisions/0012-ai-troubleshoot-sql-channel.md, and LinkedQueryTab troubleshoot — which must now be US-025, NOT US-024. Finally khuym:compounding, which has not run since ai-sql-generation on 2026-07-01 and now has five features uncaptured.
planning_notes: Mode: high_risk_feature. 5 intake flags (external systems, public contracts, existing behavior, weak proof, multi-domain) + the External-provider hard gate. Precedent: US-018 is the one AI story shipped as a high-risk story folder.

Q1 ANSWERED: LinkedQueryTab needs no schema picker. SCHEMA_INTROSPECT (db-handlers.ts:661-700) already returns every non-system schema for a database, tagged per table, capped LIMIT 5000. Plan adds a main-side fetchDatabaseSchema() mirroring that filter+cap and adding data_type + FK edges.

Q2 ANSWERED: one new channel ai:troubleshoot-sql with a discriminated payload. Extending ai:check-sql is wrong (its payload has no federated/linked variant; it is a pre-run review with no error to reason about). parseCheckResponse() (client.ts:78-125) is reusable verbatim and already makes fixedSql optional, which satisfies D4 for free.

Q3 ANSWERED: no input token ceiling exists to copy. fetchSchemaTables()/fetchForeignKeys() have no LIMIT, so ai:generate-federated-sql is ALREADY unbounded on shipped code. Troubleshoot inherits, does not worsen. Capping is deferred as separate work.

HIGH RISK FOUND: FederatedQueryTab and LinkedQueryTab have NO non-SELECT warning today (only QueryEditor does). An Apply button there could land an AI-authored DELETE silently. Safety rule: no surface gets Apply in a story that does not also give it the warning.

CORRECTED BY VALIDATING: the earlier planning claim that isReadOnlyStatement is a duplicate safe to unify was WRONG. executor.ts:92 is ^(SELECT|WITH) — a narrow C1 EXECUTION GUARD used by duck-runner.ts; QueryEditor.tsx:148 is ^(SELECT|WITH|TABLE|VALUES) — a broad WARNING CLASSIFIER. executor.test.ts pins isReadOnlyStatement('TABLE t') === false. Unifying on the broad regex would widen the read-only guard; on the narrow one it would emit false warnings. Repair: src/shared/sql-statement.ts gets stripCommentsAndStrings + a NEW isNonMutatingStatement (broad); isReadOnlyStatement STAYS in executor.ts unrenamed. A mutation-checked test must pin the two apart.

CORRECTED BY VALIDATING: the comment 'Copied from QueryEditor.tsx and kept in sync' sits above applyAutoLimit (executor.ts:97-100), NOT isReadOnlyStatement. The applyAutoLimit copies ARE byte-identical; deduplicating them is unrelated scope and was not added to US-020.
CONSTRAINT FROM VALIDATING: parseCheckResponse must gain an `export` keyword before the A1 unit test in validation.md can exist at all.

PROOF BAR: typecheck + test + build ONLY. npm run lint is declared in package.json but eslint is NOT installed (re-verified this session). Naming lint would repeat the exact failure recorded in critical-patterns.md.
Decision record required before implementation; next free integer must be checked, not assumed (docs/decisions/ has two 0006-* and two 0007-* files).

ID COLLISION CORRECTED: US-020 evidence text says "LinkedQueryTab deferred to US-024". US-024 was free in the matrix and is now taken by federated AI refine. LinkedQueryTab troubleshoot moves to US-025. The stale sentence survives inside US-020 evidence; harness-cli story update exposes no --notes, and rewriting the whole evidence string to fix one clause was judged worse than recording the correction here.

US-024 reused AI_GENERATE_FEDERATED_SQL rather than adding a channel, following the AI_GENERATE_SQL precedent where one channel carries both generate and refine via an optional baseSql. No decision record was written: the channel, the payload direction, and the refine wording all already existed; only the federated surface was missing.
US-024 did NOT touch LinkedQueryTab. Each linked-query step hosts its own SqlEditor and would need the same aiSelRef treatment per step — separate scope.

parked_features.parked_reason: User chose to implement federated AI refine first and leave troubleshoot pending UAT.
blockers: A2 STILL UNPROVEN (was a blocking spike; user chose to implement first): does a real provider return parseable JSON when the user message carries a raw multi-line Postgres/DuckDB error? Mitigated, not answered: parseCheckResponse degrades to { ok:false, summary:<raw text> } on unparseable prose rather than throwing, so the worst case is a diagnosis with no fix — never a crash. First real click is the test.

A3 STILL UNPROVEN: does a connection error yield a response with NO fixedSql? The PARSER side is now proven by client.test.ts (empty/whitespace/missing all become undefined). What is unproven is the MODEL side: whether it obeys the prompt's 'set fixedSql to an empty string' instruction for a connection failure. If it invents a fix, the Apply button appears where it should not.

Runtime UAT owed: launch the app, fail a query in both QueryEditor and Federated, click Troubleshoot, confirm Apply writes to the editor and does NOT run, and confirm a non-SELECT fix raises the warning.
parked_features.resume_with: Runtime UAT (QueryEditor + FederatedQueryTab error alert -> Troubleshoot -> Apply), then docs/decisions/0012-ai-troubleshoot-sql-channel.md, then LinkedQueryTab troubleshoot as US-025.
